key_points: Closure Property: Integers are closed under addition, subtraction, and multiplication
Commutative and Associative properties do not apply to subtraction and division
Distributive Property: $a \times (b + c) = ab + ac$ is used for simplifying expressions
BODMAS/PEMDAS rule must be followed for multiple operations
Applications involve calculating net change in temperature, elevation, or finance
examples: Real-life: Temperature at $-10^\circ\text{C}$ rising by $15^\circ\text{C}$ results in $5^\circ\text{C}$
formulas: Distributive Law $\rightarrow a(b + c) = ab + ac$
exam_tips: Use the distributive property to make large multiplications easier (e.g., $99 \times 5$ as $(100-1) \times 5$)
Always solve parentheses first in complex integer expressions
Check closure: $5 \div 2 = 2.5$ (not an integer), so division is not closed
Watch out for double negatives in word problems (e.g., 'descending' a 'negative' depth)
